data.title: Meta Platforms (META) to Lay Off 10% of Reality Labs Staff
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) plans to cut 10% of the employees in its Reality Labs division, which houses work on the company's metaverse.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) was first to report the Reality Labs job cuts, which come as the company shifts its priorities to building artificial intelligence (AI) models and applications. The cuts to Reality Labs, which had 15,000 employees at the end of 2025, are a tiny percent of Meta's total workforce of 78,000 staff.

The job cuts, which will impact about 1,500 people, are likely to hit staff who work on Meta Platforms' virtual reality headsets and a virtual reality-based social network. The metaverse is a virtual reality realm where people interact with one another using Meta's Quest series of headsets.

Budget Cuts at Meta Platforms

Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg asked senior executives late last year to make cuts to their 2026 budgets, freeing up more money for AI research and engineering. Meta has increased the amount of money it is pouring into building AI superintelligence.

The company is projected to spend $109 billion on AI-related projects in 2026. Meta Platforms reportedly plans to hit the brakes on building virtual reality products for the metaverse. Meta's Reality Labs unit has lost nearly $75 billion to date as consumer interest in virtual reality remains tepid at best.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 44 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 37 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $824.71 implies 28.47% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Citigroup Is Set to Cut 1,000 Jobs this Week
data.description:
Citigroup (C) is set to cut about 1,000 jobs this week as CEO Jane Fraser continues her plan to lower costs and improve the bank's performance. People familiar with the matter told Bloomberg that the layoffs are part of a larger plan announced two years ago to eliminate 20,000 jobs by the end of 2026. Citigroup had 227,000 employees at the end of September.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Since becoming CEO in 2021, Fraser has been working to simplify and streamline the bank by selling off much of Citi's international retail business and restructuring its main operations. In a statement, Citigroup said it will continue reducing headcount in 2026 as it adjusts staffing levels, uses more technology, and moves forward with its transformation plans. This is a busy week for the bank, as it is also set to report full-year earnings and announce employees' 2025 bonus amounts.

Looking ahead, CFO Mark Mason previously said that Citi expects its workforce to fall to about 180,000 employees by 2026, which includes 40,000 jobs that will disappear when its Mexican retail unit, Banamex, is spun off through an IPO. That means Citi will still need to cut several thousand more jobs and complete the Banamex listing to hit its goal. However, shares dropped 3% on Monday after President Donald Trump called for a cap on U.S. credit card interest rates.

Is Citigroup Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on Citigroup stock based on 13 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average Citigroup price target of $125.50 per share implies 6.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY, QQQ Shrug Off Powell's DOJ Investigation ahead of Key Inflation Data
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) reversed early-morning losses driven by the Department of Justice's investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In addition, SPY was able to secure a new intraday record high of $696.09 toward the end of the trading session.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a video statement, Powell pushed back against the investigation and said that the threat of criminal charges stems from the Fed setting interest rates independently and based on economic data rather than following President Trump's views. Trump has repeatedly called on the central bank to lower rates while leveling criticism against Powell for delaying rate cuts.

Trump has denied any role in the investigation. "I don't know anything about it, but he's certainly not very good at the Fed, and he's not very good at building buildings," he told NBC News.

The investigation could lead to "highly negative consequences," according to a joint statement released by a group of economists that includes every single living former Fed Chair and several former Treasury Secretaries. The consequences, which include a loss of Fed independence and the risk of rising inflation, mirror previous scenarios seen in emerging markets.

Furthermore, JPMorgan warned that risks to the Fed's autonomy could result in "near-term underperformance" for U.S. stocks. At the same time, a buying opportunity could emerge if the situation de-escalates amid a resilient economic backdrop and strong corporate fundamentals, said Andrew Tyler, JPMorgan's Global Head of Market Intelligence.

Meanwhile, Trump warned that the U.S. could take a trillion-dollar hit if the Supreme Court rules to overturn his tariffs. "In other words, if the Supreme Court rules against the United States of America on this National Security bonanza, WE'RE SCREWED!" he said in a Truth Social post. The Supreme Court could announce a decision in the case as early as Wednesday. Last week, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said that the Trump administration has already begun looking into legal alternatives to impose tariffs if SCOTUS strikes down the duties.

Looking ahead, the Bureau of Labor Statistics will release December's inflation data on Tuesday. Analysts expect the Consumer Price Index (CPI) to rise by 0.3% month-over-month and 2.7% year-over-year. Core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, is expected to increase by 0.3% over the prior month and 2.7% over the past year as well.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.16% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.08%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Comcast Stock (NASDAQ:CMCSA) Gains as Verizon Becomes Less Valuable
data.description:
Entertainment giant Comcast (CMCSA) has a bit of a problem, particularly in terms of its internet connectivity. The problem is that it depends far too heavily on Verizon (VZ) infrastructure to bring it to customers. But Comcast is working on building an infrastructure that counts on Verizon's a lot less. This is giving Comcast a boost with shareholders, as shares climbed over 2.5% in the closing minutes of Monday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Comcast has been investing heavily in network building, bringing in not only small cell systems, but also putting to use Citizens Broadband Radio Service (CBRS) bandwidth to help improve its operations. This is improving Comcast's wireless business, and delivering value. Recently, Comcast's CEO Brian Roberts revealed that 60% of Comcast's mobile macro network traffic comes from just 3% of its geography, meaning a lot of traffic is going on in a very confined area.

Comcast has ramped up its building in recent years, turning to Samsung (SSNLF) for its 5G CBRS Strand Small Cell system. With this system, Comcast can access the entire signal processing chain using an antenna interface. Better yet, Samsung's systems focus on "thermal management," reports note, which is vital considering the bulk of the system operates outside in the full range of temperatures that suggests.

Looking Up

Comcast's efforts have not gone unnoticed, as Bank of America recently upgraded Comcast stock from Neutral to Buy. Further, Bank of America also hiked its price target on Comcast from $31 to $37. One of the biggest reasons is that Comcast's NBCUniversal arm is poised to add new value to Comcast as media consolidation ramps up.

Bank of America shifted its valuation, noting that it now values Comcast on a "sum-of-its-parts" basis. This is a bit of a sea change, as Bank of America has previously suggested that Comcast's media assets are hurt by a "conglomerate discount." Though Bank of America does still believe this is the case, Comcast's recent spin-off of Versant (VSNT) could further narrow that conglomerate effect.

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on seven Buys, 12 Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 22.17% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $33.13 per share implies 14.92% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Anthropic Introduces Cowork to Make its Claude Code Product Easier to Use
data.description:
Amazon-backed (AMZN) AI firm Anthropic has introduced a new tool called Cowork that's designed to make its Claude Code product easier to use, especially for people who are not programmers. More specifically, the tool lets users select a folder on their computer that Claude can read from or edit, thereby making it possible to work on files through a simple chat interface. Anthropic says that this setup allows people to complete coding and file-related tasks as easily as chatting with a coworker.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What makes Cowork different is how much control it gives Claude to carry out tasks on its own. Instead of just responding to messages, Claude can plan out what needs to be done, work through the steps, and update the user along the way. Interestingly, Anthropic explained that Cowork is built on the same foundation as Claude Code, which means that it can handle many of the same jobs, but in a way that feels more natural and approachable.

This makes it useful not just for coding but also for other work that involves managing files and projects. The company said that Cowork is now available in a research preview through its macOS app to Claude Max subscribers. It's worth noting that Cowork builds on the success of Claude Code, which has become one of the company's most popular products. The new tool also arrives as Anthropic continues to grow rapidly. In fact, the company is reportedly close to raising $10 billion in a new funding round that would value it at about $350 billion.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 46 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $294.69 per share implies 19.2% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.description:

data.title: Trump Administration Edges Closer to Taiwan Tariff Deal
data.description:
The Trump administration is advancing toward the final stages of a trade deal with Taiwan that would see the island's tariff rate reduced to 15% from 20% and Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) agreeing to build at least five semiconductor fabs in Arizona, according to The New York Times. A framework of the deal could be announced as soon as this month.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Taiwan Semiconductor already has one semiconductor fab running in Arizona, with a second facility expected to be completed in 2028.

U.S. Weighs 15% Tariff for Taiwan, Matching Asian Allies

The proposed 15% tariff rate on Taiwan's imports matches the duties negotiated with Japan and South Korea, while China's rate stands at roughly 30%. Taiwan Semiconductor's investment in the U.S. is a key part of the deal as the Trump administration seeks to bolster domestic chipmaking.

In 2024, the U.S. exported $42.5 billion of goods to Taiwan and imported $116.3 billion of goods from it, resulting in a U.S. trade deficit of $73.7 billion.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Prepare for Zero Rate Cuts in 2026, Says JPMorgan
data.description:
A resilient labor market and the risk of inflation returning have swayed JPMorgan to forecast zero Fed rate cuts in 2026. In addition, the firm expects the central bank to raise rates by 25 bps during the third quarter of 2027.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The December labor report eased concerns about a slackening jobs market," said JPMorgan. The Bureau of Labor Statistics reported an unemployment rate of 4.4% in December, below the estimate of 4.5%. In addition, it revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

JPMorgan Sees Core CPI Staying Above 3% in 2026

JPMorgan believes that the core Consumer Price Index (CPI), which excludes volatile food and energy prices, will stay above 3% this year alongside jobs and gross domestic product (GDP) growth. Lower rates could fuel inflation, as they decrease borrowing costs and stimulate spending.

The firm's forecast is at odds with investor expectations, with CME's FedWatch tool pricing in a 32.6% chance of two rate cuts by year-end and a 25.4% chance of one cut.

data.title: TSMC Is About to Report Its Q4 Earnings This Week. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) or TSMC, the world's leading contract chip maker, is set to release its full fourth-quarter earnings results on Thursday, January 15. TSM stock has gained about 61% over the past year, primarily due to strong demand for advanced chips, rising orders from key clients like Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA), and improving chip supply conditions. Wall Street analysts expect the company to report earnings of $2.85 per share, representing a 30% increase year-over-year.

data.title: CoreWeave Stock (CRWV) Jumps 13% After CEO Responds to Critics
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) stock was up 13% on Jan. 12 after CEO Michael Intrator responded to criticisms about the company's business model.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Speaking on the Big Technology Podcast, Intrator shot down arguments about the useful life of graphics processing units (GPUs) that are used in artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, a concern that has weighed on the stocks of AI infrastructure companies such as CoreWeave.

CRWV stock is also up after a Moody's Ratings (MCO) report projected that AI data center investments will top $3 trillion over the next five years. The ratings firm identified CoreWeave among six U.S. companies expected to collectively invest $500 billion in data centers this year alone.

CoreWeave's Partnership with Nvidia

CoreWeave also recently announced plans to add Nvidia's (NVDA) Rubin AI microchip and related hardware to its AI cloud-computing platform in the second half of this year, positioning it among the first cloud providers to deploy the platform for customers building and running large-scale AI workloads.

Some analysts have criticized CoreWeave and its business model that's focused on developing AI data centers and then renting them out to other companies, mostly technology hyperscalers. But in a new note, investment bank Goldman Sachs (GS) said that CoreWeave's "purpose-built architecture" provides "a significant competitive advantage" for the company.

Is CRWV Stock a Buy?

CoreWeave's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 25 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 12 Buy and 13 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average CRWV price target of $123.23 implies 36.22% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
